items.title: India steps up maritime security for vessels and seafarers in West Asia
items.summary: India is actively monitoring the maritime security situation in West Asia. The government has enhanced measures to protect Indian vessels and seafarers. This comes as tensions remain high in the Persian Gulf region. Over 23,000 Indian seafarers are currently in the Gulf. Thirty-seven Indian-flagged vessels are also in the area.

items.title: WTO meet: India pushes tech transfer to boost developing nations’ trade
items.summary: New Delhi highlighted persistent barriers faced by developing economies and LDCs in acquiring, adapting and deploying critical technologies, including export controls on inputs such as semiconductor chips and rare earth minerals, as well as rigid intellectual property regimes.

items.title: New rules classify highway project failures as catastrophic; to affect contractor ratings & future contracts
items.summary: New rules define catastrophic failures in highway projects. These failures will impact contractor ratings and future contract awards. Incidents like bridge collapses or pavement failures requiring full replacement are now classified as catastrophic. Implementation agencies must report such events promptly. The National Highways Authority of India has awarded a major ring road project in Odisha to three concessionaires for development.

items.title: ET Graphics: Green energy growth enters fast lane
items.summary: India's renewable energy surge, driven by solar power, is accelerating towards its 500 GW goal by 2030.  With approximately 35 GW of solar capacity added this fiscal year, the nation is witnessing rapid expansion, robust grid planning, and manufacturing advancements. This growth is crucial for meeting rising energy demand and decarbonisation targets.

items.title: India-UK trade deal faces challenges amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: India's new trade deal with the UK faces a potential hurdle. The ongoing West Asia crisis is impacting sea routes. Vessels are rerouting away from key passages. This could affect India's exports in the initial phase of the agreement. Gem and jewellery exports are also becoming costlier due to air cargo detours. The government is closely monitoring the situation.

items.title: Banks seek clarity on study loans for GIFT City campus
items.summary: Banks are asking the government for clear rules on education loans for students at foreign universities in GIFT City. These courses are completed in India, but loans are treated as 'studies abroad'. Lenders want to update the education loan scheme. This includes removing visa requirements for loan approval. The issue was discussed internally and flagged to the government.

items.title: Railways may absorb Rs 90,000 cr bullet train cost overrun
items.summary: Indian Railways will use government funds to cover the increased cost of the Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project. The project's cost has risen significantly due to land acquisition delays. No new loans from Japan International Cooperation Agency or other institutions are planned. The government has allocated funds for the project's implementation.

items.title: As safe-haven gold surges again, loan demand to also shoot up
items.summary: Gold loan companies expect a significant increase in demand. Record gold prices, fueled by geopolitical tensions, are driving this trend.  MSMEs and individuals are likely to seek quick, collateral-backed loans.  This will help manage inventory, operational expenses, and cash flow.  Rising gold prices enhance borrowing capacity.  Gold loans are a growing retail loan category.

items.title: NDAP overhaul in works to handle surge in big data
items.summary: India's National Data and Analytics Platform is set for a major upgrade. The government plans to enhance its capacity to manage vast amounts of data. This will enable advanced analytics and the use of generative AI. The platform aims to support evidence-based policymaking. A private partner will be selected to design and build the improved system.

items.summary: Fino Payments Bank's interim head Ketan Merchant stated the arrest of CEO Rishi Gupta will not affect the bank's transition to a small finance bank.  He confirmed no abnormal impact on deposits. The issue involves program managers linked to the real money gaming business.  The bank continues proactive engagement with the RBI.  Customer balances and business volumes remain stable.

items.title: Sony Pictures reorganising units to boost regional market growth
items.summary: Sony Pictures Networks India is embarking on a transformative journey by integrating all regional TV and content ventures into a cohesive unit. This initiative is designed to amplify success in linguistically targeted markets. With the green light from unsecured creditors, the merger of Bangla Entertainment and Culver Max is set to strengthen the company’s framework and enhance operational synergy.

items.title: Dixon, Longcheer finalise JV pact to boost local electronics manufacturing
items.summary: Dixon Technologies has finalized its joint-venture with Longcheer Intelligence. The new entity, Dixtel Infocomm, will manufacture smartphones, tablets, AI PCs, and smartwatches. Dixon will hold a 74% stake, investing ₹7.39 crore. Longcheer will hold the remaining 26%. This collaboration aims to secure production volumes for Dixon. The joint venture was approved by the government and is now operational.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki to rev up its capacity next fiscal to meet surge in demand
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki is gearing up for a major leap in production, eyeing an additional 500,000 units per year to combat a swelling order backlog fueled by robust domestic sales. The activation of new plants in Haryana and Gujarat marks a pivotal shift towards meeting the escalating demand for vehicles across various categories.

items.title: Goldman Private Credit Chief Says Gating Is Feature, Not Bug
items.summary: One of Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s private credit chiefs said limits on fund withdrawals are “features and not bugs,” as the $1.8 trillion market comes under increasing pressure from investors looking to exit.

items.title: DoT seeks legal clarity on dues of insolvent telcos
items.summary: The Department of Telecommunications is seeking legal guidance on adjusted gross revenue dues for companies like RCom and Aircel. This follows a Supreme Court order preventing the transfer or sale of spectrum by these firms undergoing insolvency. Clarity is needed on how AGR dues will be handled and how spectrum will be recovered.

items.title: Ahead of its time, a GLP-1 drug almost made it out of India
items.summary: Fifteen years back, Zydus Lifesciences introduced ZYOG1, an oral GLP-1 agonist that sparked excitement for its implications in diabetes management and weight loss. Unfortunately, due to prohibitive clinical trial costs and a skeptical outlook on the obesity market, Zydus chose to shelve further development.

items.title: Trump, in Abrupt Reversal, to Continue Fight Against Law Firms
items.summary: The Trump administration has changed its mind on its decision to walk away from appealing its loss in lawsuits over executive orders targeted at four Big Law firms.

items.title: Global tech companies deepen roots within own Indian bases
items.summary: Global giants like Google, Apple, and Airbus are deepening their roots in India. They are expanding within their current campuses, not leasing new spaces. This shows a strong, long-term belief in India's capabilities. India is becoming a major hub for global operations and engineering. This trend is set to continue, with more jobs and growth expected.

items.title: West Asia conflict hits Surat textile sector, shipping costs surge 400%
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in West Asia is severely affecting Surat's textile industry. Shipping costs have surged by 400 percent. Payments are delayed as materials remain stuck on ships. Raw material prices are also climbing. This situation threatens both international and domestic supply chains. Industry leaders anticipate a significant revenue crisis. The disruption impacts trade routes to Africa as well.

items.title: Pay DTH base shrinks 12.4% to 51 million in December quarter
items.summary: India's pay direct-to-home sector continues to shrink. The subscriber base fell significantly. Internet protocol television is seeing increased activity. Private FM radio channels have decreased in number. Advertising revenue for these channels also dropped. Cable distribution operators remain registered. Satellite television channels are available for downlinking.

items.title: Mining cooperation shaping India-Argentine partnership
items.summary: Argentina and India are strengthening economic ties. Mining cooperation is a key focus, with Argentina offering a favorable framework for Indian investment in critical minerals.  Agreements are already in place for lithium exploration. India is a growing partner for Argentina, with bilateral trade increasing.  Future collaborations are expected at events like Bharat Steel 2026.

items.title: West Asia crisis: Centre ramps up monitoring of vessels and Indian seafarers
items.summary: India is bolstering maritime security for its vessels and seafarers in West Asia amidst escalating tensions. The Union Minister chaired a review meeting to assess the situation, with enhanced monitoring and reporting protocols activated for Indian-flagged ships.  Approximately 23,000 Indian seafarers are currently in Gulf countries, with 37 Indian-flagged vessels and over 1,100 seafarers in the Persian Gulf.

items.title: Indian airlines plan to operate 58 flights to Middle East on Wed: Govt

Indian carriers are resuming long-haul flights via alternative routes. Over 1,600 flights were cancelled due to the Middle East crisis. Airlines must communicate transparently with passengers regarding refunds and assistance.

The acceptance of reefer and hazardous cargo has been suspended in multiple Gulf states, with further disruptions affecting shipping routes between India and the Upper Gulf markets. Moreover, air cargo operations are experiencing significant challenges due to airspace restrictions and flight cancellations.

items.title: Oil prices to fall after Iran operation ends, says Trump amid surge due to Middle East conflict

Concerns are high as Iran issues warnings regarding passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Middle East war unlikely to dent India’s remittance inflows significantly
items.summary: The US is now the largest source of inward remittances, accounting for 27.7% of such flows into India as of FY24, per latest data from the RBI. The UAE, which was the largest source in FY17, is now the second largest, while nations like Saudi Arabia and Kuwait slipped further down.

items.title: India-EU FTA draft clarifies textile origin rules, boosts export prospects
items.summary: The draft rules specify that, for textiles, generally up to 10% of basic textile materials by weight can be non-originating, meaning they can be imported or sourced from outside the country. In certain specified cases, this tolerance can go up to 20% or 30%, depending on the product.

items.title: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms
items.summary: Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion.

items.title: Cognizant’s Ravi Kumar snaps up a deal he once won for Infosys
items.summary: Cognizant won a multi-year deal of more than $300 million from Daimler Trucks. Kumar was instrumental in winning the Daimler contract for Infosys during his stint at India's second-largest software services provider.

items.title: India’s strong core can extend goldilocks ride: RBI's Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that India's economy is healthy and robust, with broad-based growth and increased investment. He anticipates interest rates will remain stable or decrease due to benign inflation.  While acknowledging global uncertainties, Malhotra expressed confidence in the economy's sustained 'Goldilocks phase'.

items.title: Sunil Mittal unveils succession plan as Airtel raises Arpu ambition to  ₹350

items.title: BYD February Vehicle Sales Plunge 41% on Waning Domestic Demand
items.summary: BYD Co. reported a 41% drop in vehicle sales for February from a year earlier, after a record-long Lunar New Year holiday brought production and retail activity in China to a near standstill for much of the month.

items.title: Amazon cuts seller fees, again; escalates fee war with Meesho, Flipkart
items.summary: As the zero-commission model gains momentum in small towns and rural markets, Amazon is leaning on lower commissions for cheaper goods to attract small sellers to the platform.&nbsp;&nbsp;

items.title: Iran-Israel-US war: Ships asked to bypass West Asia ports
items.summary: Indian ships are told to avoid West Asian ports and the Strait of Hormuz. Major shipping companies have suspended bookings and rerouted vessels. The Strait of Hormuz is closed. This is a precautionary measure amid regional conflict. About 25 Indian vessels are being monitored.

items.title: Oil, trade risks for India amid Iran-Israel war
items.summary: Escalating Iran-Israel tensions threaten India's energy security, potentially raising oil prices and inflation. Disruptions in West Asia, a crucial $178.5 billion trading bloc for India, could impact the rupee and force exporters to find new routes. The Strait of Hormuz, vital for energy imports, remains a key chokepoint.

items.title: India's onion exports to West Asia come to a halt amid escalating tensions
items.summary: Indian onion exports to West Asia have stopped. Major ports in the region closed operations on Sunday. This has suspended vessel movements. Consignments are now stranded. The disruption occurs during the Ramadan shipping window. Overseas demand typically surges by 30% at this time. Farmers in northern Maharashtra face subdued prices.

items.title: West Asia tensions may squeeze margins in energy-intensive sectors
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are raising concerns about energy costs for Indian companies. While overall energy expenses are low, sectors like cement, glass, and transportation face higher risks. These industries could see profit margins squeezed if global energy prices surge. The situation is being closely watched as it may affect current cost trends.

items.title: No escape for the rupee also, it could slip below 91.50/$
items.summary: The Indian rupee is poised for a fall below 91.50 against the US dollar on Monday. Global oil prices and geopolitical tensions are driving this weakening. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to intervene to stabilize the currency. This comes after a period of revival for the rupee in February.

items.title: Netflix’s Co-CEO Explains Why He Quit the Warner Bros. Fight
items.summary: When Netflix Inc. dropped out of the bidding for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. on Feb. 26, the news came as a surprise to many in Hollywood.

items.title: Stability, reforms aid India's rise: Former RBI governor Shaktikanta Das
items.summary: India's economic strength stems from stable policies and reforms. The nation is poised for significant growth, contrasting with global challenges. With a young workforce and digital advancements, India is set to influence global economic trends. This progress is a result of strategic government and RBI actions, aiming for developed economy status by 2047.

items.title: Imports power growth: February GST collections rise 8.1% to ₹1.84 lakh crore
items.summary: India's Goods and Services Tax collection saw a significant rise of 8.1 percent in February, reaching ₹1.84 lakh crore. This growth was fueled by strong domestic demand and increased revenue from imports. Experts highlight this as a sign of economic recovery and the positive impact of GST reforms. The data indicates a resilient Indian economy with steady consumption.

items.title: Iran conflict threatens India’s $4.5 billion electronics exports to Gulf via UAE, Hormuz route disruptions
items.summary: India's electronics and tech exports to the Gulf face potential disruption due to the Iran conflict. The UAE, a major buyer, has seen its infrastructure targeted. Shipments to the UAE and Saudi Arabia, crucial markets for Indian electronics, rely on routes through or near Iranian airspace. This situation is being closely monitored by the government.

items.title: CBIC introduces deferred customs duty payment facility for manufacturers-importers
items.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs is launching a new facility for manufacturer importers. Approved companies can now defer customs duty payments. This move aims to boost cash flow and support domestic manufacturing. The scheme will be operational from April 1, 2026, to March 31, 2028. Eligible firms will pay duties monthly instead of at clearance.

items.title: West Asia conflict may spike dal prices; rice exports to Iran at risk

Bullion prices are expected to remain volatile as investors seek safe-haven assets.
